title: While the Seat Rumours Swirl, Ferrari Have Already Been Speaking to a Former Champion Contender
caption: Ferrari Garage
body:
With Ferrari’s driver lineup under growing scrutiny, it has emerged that the Scuderia have already been in direct contact with Liam Dela Cruz — the German driver who spent two seasons at the team before stepping away from Formula 1 earlier this year. The contact, sources say, was personal and came from within the upper levels of the organisation.

According to those sources, Ferrari put a structured proposal to Dela Cruz in the weeks following his retirement — one that centred on his continued involvement with the team. The proposed arrangement was substantial. It reportedly included appearances at selected Grand Prix weekends, involvement in Ferrari sponsor and media campaigns, access to the factory simulator programme, and a seat at the table for private Ferrari events and internal projects.

By most standards in the post-career landscape of a Formula 1 driver, it would have been an attractive package. Ferrari are one of the most commercially powerful brands in the sport, and the kind of continued access the role offered is not something teams routinely extend to departing drivers. The implication was clear: they wanted Dela Cruz’s face, reputation, and connection to the paddock to remain part of the Scuderia’s broader world even if he was no longer behind the wheel.

He said no.

“He appreciated the offer, but he made it clear he wants to step away from Formula 1 completely for now,” one source familiar with the discussions said.

The rejection has reportedly surprised several figures across the paddock. Dela Cruz spent two seasons at Ferrari — sixth in the 2024 championship with 158 points, then runner-up in 2025 with 226 — and his relationship with the team was understood to be strong. Many inside the sport had assumed Ferrari would be among the very few organisations he might remain connected to after stepping away. The speed and apparent firmness of the decline has changed that assumption.

Ferrari have not commented on the approach. What is clear is that Dela Cruz, whose popularity with fans and standing in the paddock remained high at the point of his retirement, has opted for a clean break. Whether that remains the case over the longer term is a question no one in the paddock currently has an answer to.
